assertions.expectation: Explains that GKE's maximum upgrade parallelism is approximately 20 nodes simultaneously, regardless of maxSurge setting
assertions.reason: The response suggests configuring surge settings for faster upgrades but doesn't mention the ~20 node parallelism limit that GKE actually enforces

assertions.expectation: Calculates that at 20-node parallelism, upgrading 3,000 nodes requires ~150 batches — far more than an 8-hour window allows
assertions.reason: No mathematical calculation is provided showing the batch requirements or time constraints based on GKE's parallelism limits

assertions.expectation: Recommends splitting upgrades across multiple maintenance windows (weekends) rather than attempting all at once
assertions.reason: The response suggests staggering node pool upgrades and mentions extending windows, though not explicitly across multiple weekends

assertions.expectation: Suggests upgrading CPU pools and GPU pools separately, prioritizing CPU pools first as they're less disruptive
assertions.reason: The response suggests prioritizing GPU pools first ('Prioritize GPU pools first (smaller, more critical)') which contradicts the expectation of CPU pools first

assertions.expectation: Recommends upgrading GPU pools during scheduled gaps between training/inference workloads
assertions.reason: The response doesn't mention coordinating GPU pool upgrades with training/inference workload schedules

assertions.expectation: Suggests expanding the maintenance window or using multiple consecutive windows for large pool upgrades
assertions.reason: The response explicitly suggests extending the maintenance window from 8 hours to 16-20 hours

assertions.expectation: Mentions that cluster autoscaler can only scale one node pool at a time — this interacts with upgrade scheduling
assertions.reason: The response doesn't discuss cluster autoscaler limitations or how they interact with upgrade scheduling

assertions.expectation: Addresses the 15,000 node standard limit and notes GKE 1.31+ supports up to 65,000 for AI workloads
assertions.reason: The response doesn't mention GKE node limits or the higher limits available in newer versions for AI workloads
